topic: Critical valid-proxy count gamma_c for fortified proximal causal inference: for the discrete fortified-PCI model of Rakshit-Shi-Tchetgen Tchetgen (arXiv:2506.13152) with K candidate treatment proxies of cardinality m, latent confounder of cardinality u, and binary A, define gamma_c(K,m,u) as the least gamma at which, for the WORST-CASE valid set |alpha*|=gamma, some strictly positive law makes the finite pairing matrix M_gamma(P;alpha*) between Supply = H_gamma + L2(Z_comp) and Demand = L2(U,A,Z_comp) full rank, where H_gamma = {d in L2(Z,A) : E[d|Z_-alpha]=0 for all |alpha|=gamma}; conjecture (i) a GENERIC-RANK COUNTING LAW, rank M_gamma = min(dim Supply, t) generically so feasibility reduces to the arithmetic inequality D(K,m,gamma) >= u*2*m^(K-gamma) and gamma_c is a decidable arithmetic threshold (closed form for D delegated); (ii) a UNIVERSAL-OVER-LAWS IMPOSSIBILITY, that below gamma_c fortified completeness fails at EVERY law so the anchor's Theorem 1 outcome-bridge route is inapplicable for the entire model class (the model-wide converse and the Assumption-7 dual threshold delegated); and (iii) a FINITE POLYNOMIAL-PROGRAM FORMULATION of the sub-threshold sharp identified set for the ATE via the canonical response-function reduction, with linear objective and cell-matching equalities, degree-2 conditional-independence equalities per fixed alpha*, and the union over valid sets discharged by min/max enumeration over the feasible family V_feas -- resolving whether the fortified robustness level an applied analyst is willing to defend can identify the ATE at all, which arXiv:2506.13152 leaves as an unguarded sensitivity sweep over gamma

open_problems.open_problem: For the finite categorical fortified outcome-bridge model, determine whether every worst-case valid-set pairing matrix generically has rank equal to the minimum of its supply and demand dimensions, and derive the resulting arithmetic threshold gamma_c(K,m,u).

source_refs.claim: The paper defines H_gamma and works out only a K=2, ternary-proxy, binary-U example in which completeness becomes a 12-by-15 matrix rank condition.
source_refs.author: Myeonghun Yu, Xu Shi, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: The paper gives necessary-and-sufficient subset-consistency conditions and a majority-validity sufficient rule in a canonical linear proximal SEM, not a nonparametric finite-support threshold.
source_refs.author: Prabrisha Rakshit, Xu Shi, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: the named focal scalar (critical completeness) is an operator-theoretic limit
The largest open soundness issue is finite bridge-envelope realization

open_problems.why_unsolved: Yu, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen reduce fortified completeness to a finite matrix condition only in one small example and otherwise treat gamma as an analyst-chosen sensitivity index. The banked critical-completeness attempt likewise stopped at an unobservable operator-spectrum scalar and did not prove finite bridge-envelope realization. A general count is obstructed by the overlapping conditional-mean-zero restrictions defining H_gamma and by the fact that feasible pairing-matrix entries obey positivity, normalization, and latent-proxy polynomial constraints, so row/column counting alone does not prove that a maximal minor is nonzero on the probability model or uniformly across worst-case valid sets.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would give a closed dimension formula for H_gamma, exhibit a nonzero maximal-minor polynomial on the strictly positive feasible model for every worst-case valid set, and state a decidable cardinality inequality defining gamma_c(K,m,u).

writing_exemplar.why: Its motivation moves directly from invalid treatment proxies to the robustness index gamma and the stronger completeness price that the threshold would make operational.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: many candidate proxies can aid proximal analysis -> some treatment proxies may violate exclusion -> fortification protects against an unknown valid subset of size at least gamma -> which gamma values can satisfy completeness in a finite categorical model?

method_exemplars.technique: Use tensor flattenings, Kruskal-type ranks, and a nonvanishing-minor argument to prove generic identifiability outside a proper algebraic exceptional set.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired theorem must similarly turn a finite latent categorical model into a polynomial rank problem and prove that its dimension-compatible rank is attained generically.

method_exemplars.technique: Use algebraic reparameterization and observable matrix/tensor rank constraints to obtain generic-identifiability criteria in a discrete latent model.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It shows how structural probability constraints can preserve or defeat naive generic-rank counting, which is the central issue for the fortified pairing matrix.

method_exemplars.technique: Use the union-model efficient influence function and multiply robust estimating equation with fortified outcome- and treatment-bridge nuisance models.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Above the arithmetic threshold this is the directly matched estimator and limit theory for the identified ATE.

open_problems.open_problem: Prove or refute that below the finite-support critical count, fortified outcome completeness fails for every strictly positive law for a worst-case valid set, making the anchor's outcome-bridge identification route universally inapplicable in that regime.

source_refs.claim: The outcome-bridge theorem identifies the ATE conditional on fortified completeness and calls that condition stronger than oracle completeness, but gives no model-wide converse.
source_refs.author: Myeonghun Yu, Xu Shi, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: A proximal causal functional may remain identified and root-n estimable without unique bridge solutions, so rank failure must be distinguished from ATE nonidentification.
source_refs.author: Jeffrey Zhang, Wei Li, Wang Miao, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.excerpt: The largest open soundness issue is finite bridge-envelope realization
the named focal scalar (critical completeness) is an operator-theoretic limit

open_problems.why_unsolved: The anchor proves sufficiency under fortified completeness but does not separate generic feasibility, universal infeasibility, and nonidentification of the causal query; the earlier banked proposal also lacked a finite realized model and a universal rank argument. A supply-versus-demand inequality may force every pairing matrix to be rank deficient, but the proof must match the exact function spaces for every positive law and worst-case valid set. Moreover, Zhang et al. show that a noninjective bridge operator does not automatically make the ATE unidentified, so the converse must be stated as failure of the anchor's route unless a separate query-level impossibility argument is supplied.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be an if-and-only-if regime theorem proving universal failure of fortified outcome completeness below gamma_c, plus either a positive-law counterexample separating route failure from ATE nonidentification or a distinct theorem proving query-level nonidentification.

writing_exemplar.why: Its explicit price-of-unknown-validity framing leads naturally to asking when that stronger completeness price is mathematically impossible to pay.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: unknown proxy validity requires the smaller space H_gamma -> the smaller space demands stronger completeness -> finite supports impose a hard rank ceiling -> below which gamma does the anchor's bridge route fail at every law?

method_exemplars.technique: Use null-space and orthogonal-range characterizations to distinguish uniqueness of an integral-equation solution from invariance of a linear functional over all solutions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: This machinery states exactly what deficient fortified rank rules out and prevents the invalid leap from bridge nonuniqueness to ATE nonidentification.

method_exemplars.technique: Characterize regular estimation and efficiency of linear functionals in nonparametric inverse problems through adjoint-range conditions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The fortified ATE is a linear functional mediated by a conditional-expectation operator, so adjoint-range failure separates impossible regular inference from mere nonuniqueness.

open_problems.open_problem: For gamma below the point-identification threshold, characterize the sharp ATE identified set in the fully finite fortified-proxy model as extrema of valid-set-specific response-function polynomial programs, and prove that feasible-valid-set enumeration gives the exact union-model bounds.

source_refs.claim: The paper recommends checking point-estimate stability across gamma, while its identification and efficiency results remain conditional on fortified bridge existence and completeness.
source_refs.author: Myeonghun Yu, Xu Shi, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: The paper derives proxy-based causal bounds without completeness and bootstrap inference for smoothed endpoints, but does not handle an unknown valid subset among candidate proxies.
source_refs.author: AmirEmad Ghassami, Ilya Shpitser, Eric Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: Discrete causal bounding problems reduce to polynomial programs whose global extrema are sharp, using response functions, dual relaxation, and spatial branch-and-bound.
source_refs.author: Guilherme Duarte, Noam Finkelstein, Dean Knox, Jonathan Mummolo, Ilya Shpitser
source_refs.venue: Journal of the American Statistical Association

source_refs.excerpt: Replace Exhibit 9.2 with a fully solved K=2 latent completion pair
program masses were not yet bound to the declared compatible-SCM fiber
Run the finite response-type feasibility LP for the displayed binary law and either exhibit the dual certificate

open_problems.why_unsolved: The anchor's sensitivity sweep remains inside a point-identified bridge model, while existing proxy bounds do not encode the disjunction over an unknown valid subset. Prior CausalSmith bridge-envelope and mediation programs also optimized algebraic arrays without proving that their masses belonged to the compatible SCM fiber or reproduced the full observed outcome law. Autobounds supplies the response-function reduction, but the fortified model is a finite union of latent models; the missing result must encode conditional independences within each component, justify finite response-function support, and prove that min/max enumeration yields sharp rather than outer bounds.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be an explicit finite polynomial program with linear ATE objective, observed-cell matching and degree-two conditional-independence equalities for each fixed valid set, plus a sharpness theorem equating union-model endpoints to the minimum and maximum over feasible-set-specific programs.

writing_exemplar.why: Its applied sensitivity narrative already asks analysts to vary gamma, making the natural pivot a formal identified set when completeness cannot hold.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: proxy validity is uncertain -> the analyst chooses a defensible lower bound gamma -> fortified point identification additionally requires completeness -> when finite-support completeness is impossible, what is the sharp ATE range?

method_exemplars.technique: Reduce a finite SCM to response-function probabilities, express estimands and assumptions polynomially, and certify sharp extrema by dual relaxation and spatial branch-and-bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The requested object is a finite sharp causal bound under latent-variable and conditional-independence restrictions, with valid-set enumeration as the extra disjunction.

method_exemplars.technique: Enumerate deterministic response types and optimize a linear causal estimand over observed-cell matching constraints to prove tight bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It is the canonical proof that response-function optimization yields sharp causal bounds; fortification adds quadratic proxy-validity constraints and a finite union.

method_exemplars.technique: Estimate nonsmooth proxy-bound endpoints through smooth approximations and construct bootstrap confidence intervals for the approximated bounds.
method_exemplars.why_closest: It supplies proxy-specific endpoint inference that can be adapted after the fortified program determines the min/max endpoint geometry.

open_problems.open_problem: For a fixed latent-support cap, characterize exactly which algebraic proximal bridge-null directions are induced by nonnegative latent negative-control laws, with a support-capped algorithm and an explicit dimension or complexity bound.

source_refs.excerpt: Strengthen Conjecture 1 beyond generic Farkas projection by giving an explicit support-capped algorithm
the promised Farkas multiplier and distinct sharp ATE sets were not computed and verified

open_problems.why_unsolved: The prior attempt unfolded fixed-support latent feasibility into a generic projection/Farkas statement, but did not characterize the image created jointly by nonnegativity, negative-control exclusions, the full observed outcome law, and bridge-null perturbations. Its proposed finite witness was arithmetically invalid, leaving no primitive criterion that separates causally realizable null rays from spurious algebraic ones. A useful result must exploit fixed support to give an explicit finite family and complexity bound rather than merely invoke abstract elimination.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would be a finite union-of-semialgebraic-cones characterization with an exhaustive support-pattern algorithm, primal latent completions for membership, dual certificates for nonmembership, and a proved dimension or complexity bound.

writing_exemplar.why: Its finite categorical negative-control model gives the cleanest path from latent stochastic tables to the gap between algebraic bridge directions and causal completions.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: categorical negative controls encode latent confounding through finite stochastic tables -> rank-deficient bridge equations create algebraic null directions -> exclusions and nonnegativity need not lift every direction -> characterize the fixed-support realizable cone

method_exemplars.technique: Use finite response-type enumeration, primal feasibility, and dual certificates to establish sharp causal restrictions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The desired object is likewise a projection of a finite causal response-type system, now with negative-control independences and a prescribed bridge-null direction.

method_exemplars.technique: Use categorical double-negative-control moments and multiply robust estimation under finite latent-table restrictions.
method_exemplars.why_closest: Its moments provide the natural estimation layer once the realizable-cone result determines which causal functionals are defined.

open_problems.open_problem: Derive the finite-support critical valid-proxy count for fortified treatment-bridge completeness, including its dependence on outcome-proxy support, and determine when it is below, equal to, or above the outcome-bridge threshold.

source_refs.claim: The treatment-bridge assumption uses L2(W,A,X)+L2(Z_comp,X) to separate L2(U,A,Z_comp,X) and is stronger than oracle treatment completeness, but no categorical threshold is given.
source_refs.author: Myeonghun Yu, Xu Shi, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

source_refs.claim: With two independent proxies satisfying a finite-support rank condition, the causal effect is identified even when the proxy measurement mechanism is not.
source_refs.author: Wang Miao, Zhi Geng, Eric J. Tchetgen Tchetgen

open_problems.why_unsolved: The treatment route has a different supply space from the outcome route: its rank ceiling depends directly on the support and conditional variation of W as well as on A and the invalid-proxy complement. The outcome-side count therefore cannot simply be reused. The anchor states the operator condition but neither derives a finite categorical dimension formula nor determines whether one fortified route can remain feasible when the other is universally impossible.
open_problems.what_a_resolution_would_look_like: A resolution would give generic-rank and universal-impossibility theorems for the treatment-bridge pairing matrix, an arithmetic threshold gamma_c^q(K,m,u,|W|), and a regime-comparison theorem relating it to the outcome-bridge gamma_c.

writing_exemplar.why: It presents outcome and treatment bridges as complementary routes, making it the natural framing for a map of where each route is feasible.
writing_exemplar.motivation_arc: proximal ATE identification has outcome- and treatment-bridge routes -> fortification shrinks their information differently -> finite supports impose route-specific rank ceilings -> which route survives at each valid-proxy count?

method_exemplars.technique: Translate categorical completeness into matrix rank and use the two-proxy latent measurement structure to identify the causal functional without identifying the full measurement mechanism.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The treatment-side fortified assumption is an enlarged finite-proxy rank problem indexed by the invalid-proxy complement.

method_exemplars.technique: Use generic tensor-rank arguments to establish when finite observed blocks contain enough variation to separate a latent categorical state.
method_exemplars.why_closest: The treatment-route threshold depends on whether W and Z_comp jointly separate U and A, which is a finite latent-block rank problem.

method_exemplars.technique: Use treatment- and outcome-bridge influence functions to construct doubly robust, locally efficient ATE estimators and derive the efficiency bound.
method_exemplars.why_closest: After identifying which route is rank-feasible, this bridge-specific influence-function theory supplies the estimator and limit law.

literature_map:
1. Yu, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2025, arXiv:2506.13152) introduce fortified bridges for an unknown valid subset and leave gamma as an analyst-set sensitivity index; the supplied topic misattributes this paper to Rakshit.
2. Their outcome-side fortified completeness trades a smaller H_gamma supply space against a stronger rank requirement, but only one K=2 categorical example is worked out.
3. Their treatment-side assumption has a distinct supply involving W and Z_comp, with no finite-support threshold or comparison of routes.
4. Rakshit, Shi, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2025, arXiv:2507.19623) study invalid treatment proxies in a canonical linear SEM and use a majority-validity rule, not the finite nonparametric count problem.
5. Miao, Geng, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2018, Biometrika, doi:10.1093/biomet/asy038) provide the finite-proxy rank-condition foundation for proximal identification.
6. Cui, Pu, Shi, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2024, JASA, arXiv:2011.08411) provide proximal efficiency bounds and doubly robust locally efficient inference.
7. Zhang, Li, Miao, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, Statistics & Probability Letters, arXiv:2303.10134) show that bridge nonuniqueness need not destroy causal-functional identification or root-n inference.
8. Ghassami, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2023, arXiv:2304.04374) obtain proxy-based partial identification without completeness and smoothed/bootstrap endpoint inference.
9. Ghassami, Ying, Shpitser, and Tchetgen Tchetgen (2022, AISTATS, arXiv:2104.02929) give minimax bridge estimation and asymptotic-linearity conditions for proximal functionals.
10. Duarte, Finkelstein, Knox, Mummolo, and Shpitser (2024, JASA, arXiv:2109.13471) establish response-function polynomial programs for sharp discrete causal bounds.
11. Balke and Pearl (1997, JASA, doi:10.1080/01621459.1997.10474074) are the canonical response-type linear-program sharp-bounds anchor.
12. Allman, Matias, and Rhodes (2009, Annals of Statistics, arXiv:0809.5032) supply generic tensor-rank tools for finite latent models.
13. Gu (2025, Bernoulli, arXiv:2203.04403) shows that algebraic constraints can make generic discrete latent identification depart from naive counting.
14. Severini and Tripathi (2012, Journal of Econometrics, doi:10.1016/j.jeconom.2012.05.018) provide adjacent operator-functional regularity and efficiency machinery.
15. The live tension is point identification conditional on fortified completeness versus the absence of an arithmetic feasibility diagnostic and a sharp union-model fallback when the defended gamma is sub-threshold.
prior_proposal_map:
Repository-wide filename and keyword screening covered 295 proposal/rejected-angle TeX files, 1,059 reviewer JSONs, and 144 bank READMEs; 22 proximal/negative-control rejected angles received closer review.
The closest qid was eid_proximal_completeness/v1: all four rejected angles, the final proposal, README, and the angle0_v3, angle3_v1, angle4_v3, and angle4_v5 reviews were inspected.
eid_proximal_completeness exposed an unobservable spectral threshold, unproved bridge-endpoint causal realization, and unproved uniform weak-proxy inference.
pid_proximal_null_realizability/v1 supplied the fixed-support latent-completion problem; both rejected angles, README, and angle0_v1-v2 reviews were inspected.
Its angle0_v2 reviewer yielded the strongest strengthening path: replace generic Farkas projection with an explicit support-capped algorithm and complexity bound.
eid_proximal_phase/v1 supplied earlier finite-realization, residual-tolerance, and selector-range attempts; its rejected angle0, final proposal, README, and review trail were screened.
scm_prox_recant_pid/v1 and pid_mediation_proxy/v1 were checked for response-function sharpness failures; their notes show missing compatible-SCM-fiber and endpoint-realizability proofs.
eid_proximal_bridge_quotient/v1 and eid_proximal_quotient_inference_phase/v1 were duplicate guards: the static null-annihilator criterion is solved, while uniform boundary inference remains open.
The current scm_fortified_proxy_threshold directory had only pipeline/state material, so no same-qid prior proposal or reviewer artifact existed.
